context.scenario:
To set the scene the day after the birth of my second child my partner explained to me that she wanted to repair the relationship with her sister that was damaged following her sisters defence of her brother who had stolen money from our house while a guest.
I accepted this saying that its her decision and I would support her however for me to forgive her it might take some time to process it before inviting the family to the house again for a family gathering which I felt would be pending.
I calmly said I needed time to process and perhaps slept on it to get to grips with it. The way I communicated this was important as she had previously told me to improve my communication skills especially around my feelings as it had previously escalated discussions in full blown arguments because I struggled to clearly communicate my feelings.
Therefore, you can imagine that following my calm explanation of my feelings her response was dismissive at best. I felt she just discarded them inferring I should grow up and get over myself and it had nothing to do with me.
At this point, I regretted my next actions as I saw red, I burst into a verbal rage saying how dare she not validate my feelings especially after I had taken on board her feedback and improving my communication skills and calmly explaining my feelings. I felt I couldn’t make her happy regardless of my communication approach.
Obviously once I had calmed down, I apologized for my outburst explaining my behaviour wasn’t acceptable and how much I regretted it. Conversely, she felt no need to apologise as she didn’t believe she had done anything wrong and furthermore my actions meant that I lost any rights for an apology. Is she correct in her opinion?
context.comment:
NTA

Let's recap: You were told that you needed to do a better job of expressing yourself. So, you expressed to your wife that it would take some time for you to forgive your in-laws after experiencing a theft. Instead of accepting your feelings, she dismissed them and implied that it "didn't affect you" when it very clearly did. After all, your BIL stole from the both of you, not just her. 

After your "outburst" (which, by the way, is understandable when she dismissed the feelings she wanted you to express), you apologized and her response was to simply accept it and not apologize herself for her dismissive actions. Your wife is 1,00% in the wrong here. You don't go around asking people to express themselves and then dismiss those expressions.

context.scenario: I'm 31f and my mom is 51. She's been dating her boyfriend for about 3 years or longer. At first I liked him, because he never gave me a reason to not like him until recent when i saw him on a dating site searching for a "long term partner." I was in complete shock. I screenshot his profile and showed it to my mom. She told me that she would confront him. I assumed she did because his profile vanished but obviously she didn't break it off because he showed up to see my brother off to prom last night. A few days before this happened I saw him on YET ANOTHER dating app looking for a long term partner. When I saw him yesterday I completely snapped on the car ride home. It ended with me telling my mom, "I guess you've gotten old and desperate." We had words back and forth and I have yet to apologize. I understand it's her life but I don't understand after all she's been through with men why she would tolerate another one who obviously doesn't want her. I feel like an ahole but another part of me feels like I'm right. 🤷‍♀️
context.comment:
YTA

So let's recap... Your mother decided to (at least from what you know of) continue to see someone who is on dating apps. Instead of MINDING YOUR OWN DAMN BUSINESS, you decided to completely disrespect your mother, who's done nothing wrong. 

You know what, I'd maybe give you a pass if you had confronted the real asshole here.... But no.... Don't say a word to him... Just call your mother OLD and "desperate"..... FFS, what is wrong with you?!?! You're THIRTYONE years old and this is how you talk to innocent people?! The fact that you have yet to apologize shows a lot about your character. You know what makes no sense at all?? You claim you feel like an asshole but you haven't said you were sorry. 

Word to the wise...... You're 31... Before you know it you're gonna be 51. You might want to check yourself and realize that 51 ain't fuckin old.

context.scenario:
This is a throwaway as friends know my main.

So I (28F) got married last year. It was a small but beautiful ceremony with our closest family and friends. My older sister (32F) and I have always had a complicated relationship, we’re close, but she’s also extremely emotional and kind of dramatic. She was supposed to be my maid of honor.

Two days before my wedding, her 14 year old dog passed away unexpectedly. I was obviously sympathetic, but she called me sobbing, saying she couldn’t emotionally handle coming to the wedding because she was too devastated. I tried to talk her down, even offered to have someone bring her home early if it was too much, but she refused and didn’t show up.

It hurt. A lot. She missed one of the biggest days of my life, and I had no maid of honor. We didn’t talk much after that. A few months ago, she got engaged and just last week asked me to be her maid of honor.

I told her I couldn’t. I said I still felt hurt she missed my wedding, and while I love her, I just couldn’t pretend like everything was fine. She got extremely upset, said I was being cold and petty, and that her dog was like her child. Our parents are pressuring me to just be the bigger person.

I honestly don’t know anymore. AITA for saying no?
context.comment:
YTA

"I was obviously sympathetic".... Uh, no.... No ya weren't. If you were sympathetic, you would have accepted that she was unable to attend your wedding to her mental health and more importantly, you wouldn't be taking it out on her a year later by refusing to be her Maid Of Honor. Seriously, what a shitty thing to do to someone you allegedly care about. Also, your parents are "pressuring you to be the bigger person", they're trying to convince you to be a GOOD PERSON and not treat your sister as if she did something wrong. 

I get it, you were upset she didn't go to your wedding. But she lost her dog that she'd had for 14 years. Even if you've experienced the same exact thing, it still doesn't give you the right to treat her like shit and pass judgement on her. Punishing someone for how they (rightly so) reacted to losing a loved one is insane to me.

context.scenario:
We’re both in our 30s. My husband has always been into anime as a hobby, and honestly, I usually just ignored it because whatever — everyone has their thing.

But the other day, he finished some show about a group of high school friends, and he literally started crying over it. Like full-on emotional, telling me about the story and characters and everything. I know it sounds mean, but it honestly felt a little off-putting to me. We’re adults — seeing him get so worked up over a cartoon about teenagers just threw me off.

Without really thinking, I told him he needed to grow up and stop watching anime. Now he won’t talk to me at all.

He said he works all week, deals with all kinds of stress, and that watching anime for an hour a day is his way of winding down. And honestly... now I feel kind of bad.

I didn’t realize how important it was to him. I just saw it as childish and didn’t get why he was so invested. Now it’s super awkward between us and I don’t really know how to fix it.
context.comment:
YTA

I am sick to death of people (mostly women) thinking watching/doing certain activities makes a person childish. IMO, it's a hell of a lot more childish to judge someone based on their interests and hobbies. Second thing about this that really pisses me off.... You find it off putting that he cried?? Why??? Cause you find the subject ridiculous?? Is it cause men shouldn't cry?? Either way, that's bullshit and makes you an ah. You mean to tell me you've NEVER in your adult life watched a fictional show or movie and cried??? Cause if you have, you're also a hypocrite !! I wonder how you'd feel if he were "put off" by seeing you cry after watching a movie like the notebook.

context.scenario:
Ok guys so I live in east Yorkshire, England. And we went on a  trip to south Wales for a couple of days, which is a 5 and a half hour drive from where I live. This is a friend I have known for a very long time. He was actually supposed to drive and agreed to because I am not comfortable driving long distances like that. But he hurt his foot at the gym the day before, and I basically had no choice but to drive. Anyway, it was all fine. We got there and had a good time. But the problem came when we left. My friend booked the hotel we stayed at, and he was the one who signed in and out. 

So over halfway home (3 hours of driving). He realises he left a bunch of his stuff at the hotel. His wallet, house keys and his own car keys. He freaked out and begged me to go back. No one else would be in his house, and he needed his car to go to work on Monday. I was very frustrated by this, of course. It messed up my own plans and meant adding 6 hours more to the drive in total. Which was exhausting (keep in mind this is driving in England, nothing like the nice open American roads). I probably did say some mean things in frustration. But I relented and did it. I drove back and we got his stuff. And after a horrible drive, we got home. Realising that I had to spend £70 on fuel because of the extra 6 hours. And I told him in no uncertain terms, I expect him to give me the money. To me, that is completely fair. He disagreed. Couldn't believe I was even asking, "that's what friends do!" he exclaimed. He shouted and argued and he stormed off. But I am not backing down. Mutual friends are now messaging me, saying I am AITA. 

AITA?
context.comment:
YTA

It was a mistake. You're acting as if he did this on purpose. I'm very curious what you mean by " I probably did say some mean things in frustration". What do you mean "probably"??.... You either did or you didn't. Something tells me that you know for sure that you said shit you shouldn't have and are purposefully leaving that part out cause you don't want us to say you're an ah. 

Here's my take.... If you were the one that forgot your stuff how would you feel?? You're stressed out cause you forgot important belongings at a hotel and to make matters worse, your friend makes you feel like shit and then demands you pay him for it.

context.scenario:
i (19F) lived with another girl in college. she consistently would eat my food and when i caught her said she would “replace it”, but never did. i finally got a mini fridge for my room and started labelling what i left in the kitchen.

she was calling me “petty” and told our friends i made the apartment “hostile” (really don’t know how it was my fault). i just don’t think i should be feeding the both of us. AITH for locking up my food?
context.comment:
NTA

But your roommate is!! Not only is she an ah for eating food that isn't hers, she's also an ah for not replacing it. Then she's an ah for calling you petty.... And again an ah for telling people outside the issue that you're making the situation hostile. If anyone is making it hostile, it's her!!

context.scenario:
I 49F have 2 adult children who both still live at home, Emma 25F and Ian 23M. Their father and I divorced 12 years ago so it’s been the 3 of us for a while now. I had received child support until they turned 18. Their father had paid it willingly so it wasn’t arranged through the courts. This is why he stopped at 18 even though they were both technically still in school. 

Fast forward to present day. They both work full time, don’t have any debt from school, and both still live at home. I have asked them repeatedly to contribute a set amount each month towards household expenses since I can’t continue supporting them forever. Ian doesn’t have a problem with this, but Emma thinks it’s my responsibility as her mother to support her financially until she’s ready to move out. I’m not asking them for much or trying to profit from them, I just want to be able to save for my retirement and be able to retire. I’d be happy with $100 each a week to cover their share of groceries, utilities, and household supplies. I buy all the groceries, laundry detergent, toilet paper, etc for the house. They are responsible for their cars and related expenses, cell phones, and personal toiletries. 

Some of my daughter’s friends live at home for free, but those friends also live in a home with both parents working. My daughter has actually said she will quit her job and go on social assistance just so she can’t afford to pay me rent. 

So internet strangers, AITA for asking my adult children to pay rent to live at home?
context.comment:
NTA

Emma needs a wakeup call!! Not only is she wrong about you supporting her "until she's ready to move out", she's also wrong in the sense that no parent is expected/required to care for their child after they turn 18. Honestly, it doesn't matter if she has friends that still live at home for free. Everyone's situation is different and IMO, allowing a 25 year old to live rent free in your home when they're working a full time job doesn't do much for their maturation.

context.scenario:
So my 15 year old son has a mother and son dinner event with his football team. I'm obviously planning to go as it'd be nice bonding time and well..im his mother. 

Today, my son told me that my ex's wife was planning to go but she didnt say a word to me about it. In all honesty, I dont want her to go. Yes, shes been in my son's life the passed like 5 years or so but shes not his mom nor did she have the respect to call/text me and ask how I felt about it. Would I be TA if I politely told her I didn't want her to go? Im just really conflicted.
context.comment:
YTA

1. It's your son's event. HE gets to choose who attends, not you. 

2. Again, it's your son's event.... If you're unsure as to how to go about this, maybe ask your son what he thinks.

3. I find it funny that you'd assume your son would not only not want her there, but also that he's okay with you making decisions like this without his input. You say "she didn't have the respect to ask". Maybe she did... Maybe she asked the person she should be asking... Your son.

context.scenario: I’m 35 she’s 42. We’ve been together since 2012. She has a son who is now 22 and a brother that’s 59 that’s just moved into in MY house. I work long hours. 4am until about 7-8pm most nights. She use to start cooking around 6 so I’ll be walking in once the food is done. They moved in in February and i noticed she started cooking early and once i get in the food looks disgusting because it’s like im eating scraps. I’ve told her that i don’t want any man in my house touching the food before i get my plate. I don’t care if it’s her grown son or her brother. That’s not how we have been doing things and i feel disrespected. She said she understands where I’m coming from since i don’t have kids, but that’s her son. I said that’s a grown man eating out of my dishes sleeping in my house eating the food i paid for. If he wants to eat-cook his own meal but don’t touch anything u cooked. I don’t feel like the …
context.comment:
YTA

Good lord, dude.... Get the fuck over yourself. Just cause it's your house doesn't entitle you to the first plate. Honestly kid.... Your wife should divorce you. No one deserves to be treated like that. Basically, what you're saying in a round about way, is that your wife is YOUR servant and is only permitted to serve YOU.

"I don't want any man eating food my wife cooked"... What an absolutely INSANE way of thinking.

context.scenario:
I am having a very big moral dilemma with this one because right now I don’t have anyone to talk to about it to see if I’m being an asshole about this. 

So for a little while now we me (M25) and my gf (F25) have been planning to go back to my home town to meet a good chunk of my family. This would include my parents, my brother and his gf, and my grandparents. The first part of the trip would be driving 5 hours to a restaurant my family went to every year when we were kids. This is a VERY important thing to me, I haven’t seen my grandparents in a long time and this was somewhere we’ve gone to since I could barely eat solid food. I haven’t been there in 5 years when we used to go every single year. We would then go stay at my parents house for the next 2 days. The second day we would go to Niagara Falls with my mom, brother, and his gf. The last day, we would be getting a trailer to grab my motorcycle from home, and bring it back home. It seemed like a pretty straightforward trip to me. 

Anyways, I come visit her after work and she casually brings it up that her friend may be coming along too. This threw me for a loop because she never asked me or brought it up to me before so I thought she was joking. It soon dawned when she asked me to tell my parents she was coming that it was actually real. She told me I have to tell my parents that she’ll be staying at my parent’s house with us. There was no talking about it, asking, or anything, just saying that’s how it is. I told her she would be the one to tell my mother that her friend was coming because she decided she wanted to invite her without telling me.

I got a bit upset because this would be there first time she would be meeting my dad, my brother, and my grandparents. It felt like a very intimate time to me especially with her going to a place that was a very big part of my childhood. She then got upset with me because she said that she didn’t think it would be a big deal to invite her friend along. She doesn’t understand why I’m acting all upset over it. I tried to explain it was important to me but she just shut down and barely talked to me. I asked if she would have invited her friend if she we weren’t going to Niagara Falls and she said no. They are both from Central America and her friend is apparently flying home the same time as the trip which she had just learned. I didn’t know this until the argument started. I told her if she wanted to we could have done something before hand if she had told me but the damage was already done. This trip is happening at the end of the month. I realize I may be the asshole in this and she told me I was overreacting but I need to talk to someone about this and I have no one. 

TLDR: My gf invited her friend on our trip to a meet my family and got upset when I didn’t like the plan.
context.comment:
NTA

This would be a good time to think about this relationship. The fact that she doesn't understand why you're upset shows you are VERY different people. Not only did she make the assumption that you'd be okay with her friend tagging along to a family dinner, she also unilaterally decided that HER friend would be staying at your parents house without even speaking to you about it. IMO, that's not gf material right there. Her actions are both inappropriate and self-centered. 

DO NOT let her or anyone else let you think you're even remotely close to being in the wrong on this. You've done NOTHING wrong!!

context.scenario:
I (28M) have a brother (34M) who has a wife and kid (2F). We live in the same country but still very far apart. It literally takes 12 hrs to get to his by train which itself is so expensive. He really wanted me to come last week because he said it will give him and his wife some time to relax and unwind, so i can play with my niece more. I did that. I was sooo exhausted on my journey there and back, i just got back last thrusday. I was out with a friend on saturday and he called me up because he said my niece wanted to see me. I told him i cant talk as im out.

Today got a call from my mom being annoyed with me because my brother told her ever since i came back (again.. LITERALLY LAST THRUSDAY) i havent called to speak to his daughter and how im being so terrible.

I got so pissed off. He has done this in the past.. one time my ex was literally in the hospital dying of cancer when he asked me to go up and visit him and that my ex has his mom who can take care of him, and then complained to mom when i said i couldnt.. i felt so guilty i ended up going for 4 days anyway. 

What really pisses me off is he barely messages to ask me how im doing, how my partner is, what stuff i have been up to.. when i was visiting him last week he expected me to take the week off but he didnt do it himself. The one night we tried to bond and watch a movie, he got tired halfway through and wanted to go to sleep (which i dont mind. Im not a clingy-unable-to-look-at-facts person, but dont do the shit to me then!) 

Also i love my niece but i have a life of my own, my job is stressful! I go out to events and hang with friends on weekends! And when i get time to myself i just wanna be alone. I DONT want to call him up every single week!! I have a life!
context.comment:
NTA

She's 2..... What's he think you're gonna do?? Spend 20 minutes per week talking to her on the phone?? You're a grown ass adult. If he wants her to see you, they can get on the train and come visit. Or hop on a plane.... Or ask you to put aside a few minutes a week to video chat.

context.scenario:
So I (24M) have been with my gf (25F) for 3 years. We are living together and very in love. We’ve discussed marriage before and are on the same page about it. I’d like to propose to her because there’s no reason to wait when we both know we want to marry each other. The problem is I really don’t have a lot of money and my gf has a good salary. I brought this up to her the other day and said that I know she has more in her bank account than I do for now but we’ll eventually have a joint bank account. Since our money will be shared anyway, I asked if she would pay for her engagement ring. I thought this made a lot of sense and wouldn’t be a problem. I was wrong. My gf seemed really offended by this and said that she wouldn’t fully buy her own ring. I don’t get this, it’s going to all be OUR money anyway, so why should it matter who pays for the ring? She’s been kind of distant with me and I’m wondering if my request was really so wrong. AITA?

EDIT:
Wow, I see everyone thinks I’m a gold digger after my gf’s money? That’s ridiculous, I love her for who she is, not her money. I’m in between jobs right now and really can’t afford the ring but we’d both like to get engaged as soon as possible and she deserves something nice. I don’t see why saying the money will be ours anyway is so bad, because it WILL. My money will be hers, too in our future joint bank account.
context.comment:
YTA

While I will say you're an ah for asking her to pay for what's basically a gift to her, I will say that I also think it's absolute bullshit that while we're told certain marriage traditions need to go, this is the one that will always stay. Why is it that it's sexist and demeaning for a man to speak to the father of his SO but it's perfectly acceptable to say that the man is required to propose?? To the point where it's still socially acceptable for a woman to hound her bf demanding he propose or end the relationship. Since she makes more money than you and wants to get married, maybe she can ask you instead!!

context.scenario:
My kitchen sink was clogged last night, so I called the landlord. He said, “I’ll send someone over to fix it first thing in the morning.” Sure enough, someone did show up—but it wasn’t a plumber like I expected. It was his son.

He looked at the sink, took a few pictures, and literally posted them on Reddit after watching a YouTube video. I asked if he was a handyman or plumber, and he said, “Nah, I’m a student studying accounting. Don’t worry, I’ll have this fixed. I’m still learning.”

He sat around for a while, then came to me and said he’d be back in an hour because he needed to buy tools from Home Depot. I told him that wouldn’t be necessary and asked him to leave my house.

I later called the landlord and told him he needs to hire a professional—not send over his son who has zero skills or qualifications to fix the issue. He responded with, “I don’t see the issue. He’s still learning and has already fixed plenty of things at my other properties without a problem. I can call a plumber in two days.”

I know he could’ve called a plumber that same day—it was still midday during that call—but he clearly chose not to, just because I hurt his son’s ego and he doesn’t want to spend a few extra bucks.
context.comment:
YTA

As far as I know, you don't get to decide who your landlord "hires" to fix issues at HIS property. It's a clogged sink, not a complicated plumbing issue. The only thing you can legally do is force him to have it fixed in a timely manor. Also, you're a grown up.... fix the damn clogged sink yourself. I can't imagine calling my landlord for such a trivial reason.

context.scenario:
I (23m) just applied to a nice apartment. After a month of trying to find the perfect place in my price range, my sister and brother in law told me about their complex. They live in a town home which is way out of my budget but the complex also had one bedrooms which were perfect for me at a great price. 

The complex doesn’t advertise on normal apartment websites as they prefer getting residents from the area and word of mouth. They also have a special that if you are referred by someone they get a 500 dollar Walmart gift card. 

I moved out from my roommates apartment and left almost all my belongings there (just part of my situation I was in months ago) leaving me with almost nothing to move with into this apartment. I have a good chunk of savings, but any help I have been getting has been a miracle. 

I asked if we could split the Walmart gift card so I could get a bunch of little things for bathroom and kitchen. My brother-in-law and sister said no as they were using it to get a new TV. I told them about my plight and they just said they would be on the lookout for some of the things I needed. 

Anyways, it got time for application and I put nobody as my referral. When I left the management office I went over to their apartment to eat dinner and celebrate. They asked if I referred them and I said I left it blank. They got kind of upset saying it took no effort or money on my part, and I was essentially helping no one in the situation. They are demanding I go back in and say I was referred but i don’t think they will let me and I don’t want to embarrass myself like that. 

They have their place established, they pay less rent because they split it, they have all the furniture. I need the help and they refused. Ya it was free and easy to do, but they couldn’t even split it for me. 

AITA?

TLDR; Refused to put sister and brother in law as referral bonus  so they missed out on their referral bonus because they wouldn’t split it with me
context.comment:
YTA

They referred you, then you asked for half of the referral they'd get.... and when they said no, you just decided to not put them down as a reference...... That's pretty petty of you. They even offered to keep an eye out for stuff you'd need.  Perhaps you shouldn't have left most of your belongings behind in your previous apartment??

context.scenario:
For context, I (26m) and my gf (25f) have been together for 4 and a half years and living together for 3. We rarely fight and all in all have a very good relationship. So good that I plan on proposing here in a month or so. 

We spend a good amount of quality time together on a daily basis (watching our show, shopping, and anything else her or I want to do) but the bulk of pressure in regards to time spent comes at night. Due to our financial situation I have to work 60+ hours a week in order to pay for our lifestyle (our mortgage, things for our dog, save up for future children, etc.) The problem lies with the fact that I work a normal Monday-Friday schedule with a lot of Saturdays being mandatory OT, but have weekends off when not, while she works Tuesday-Thursday and Sundays as a waitress until she finds a job to utilize her degree from college. Due to this, Saturday nights are typically my only nights to have a couple hours to myself doing whatever it is I like to do while she sleeps in preparation for her work day. Usually this includes video games and a couple beers until I get tired and head to bed. She is always upset at the fact that I never go to bed with her saying she has trouble sleeping alone even though I am just in the living room adjacent to our bedroom. 

This situation has been consistent and has led to tonight where we got into an argument where I decided to turn everything off and make it very apparent that she had “gotten her way” and I will just go to bed with her. Even though the 2-4 hours I spend is my only time I have to spend doing entirely what I want to do after every week of me consistently put in 60+ hours at work to provide the life that she wants. She got upset and called me an asshole for not wanting to lay with her while she sleeps and that she knows I would be upset if the shoe was on the other foot. This is entirely false and I actually encourage her to do things she enjoys if I go to bed throughout the week before her. 

I dont know. I guess I am just fed up from having the same bickering match every single week when I feel like she doesn’t understand the stress I put myself under for her to have the majority of what she has. I know I dont HAVE to do that but I want to be able to provide that for her and wish she would appreciate that enough to understand I need a couple hours a week to decompress, but I also see where she may feel upset that I would rather spend my time doing anything else than be with her as I love her dearly. 

So am I really that much of an AH for wanting some time to myself?
context.comment:
NTA

As long as you're not spending a ridiculous amount of hours playing, you're good. If she's having trouble sleeping, she needs to see a doctor. You shouldn't be required to go to bed when you're not tired because she's having trouble sleeping. You're working a shit ton of hours per week. You deserve ONE little activity that relaxes you.

context.scenario:
So, to clarify me and my family are pretty well off , my showers usually last about 20-25 minutes but I always get screamed at after leaving because apparently we should all be taking 5 minute showers.. 

Her main arguments are “what do you do in there for that long” and I can’t really say scrubbing my body , hair , exfoliating etc etc bc she literally takes 5 minute showers so she would never get it , honestly I have no idea what to do and I’m just lost in how to explain that showering is part of my time to clean and prioritize myself.
context.comment:
NTA

But I will say it can depend on how many showers you have in your house, how many people live in the house and the time of day you're showering. It's hard to not call you an ah if it's the only shower in the house and you're taking 25 minutes to shower during prime shower times and there's 4+ people in the house that need to shower.

scenario:
I live with another girl around my age (19f) currently and she has a dog. I dont really like animals and we had a problem with her dog messing up my stuff when we first moved in we dont interact much anymore so im good now. My roommate left for a week and asked me to feed her dog, again i said sure bc even though i dont really like animals im not gonna just refuse like an ass😭 until this morning i fed her and it was the last of the food in the bag so i texted my roommate to tell her and she sent me a pic of the brand and asked me to go get it from the store. I said sure and asked her to send me the money for it and she told me no and asked if i could pay for it. I told her i would if she’d pay me back for it afterwards and she said no again…so i told her i wasnt buying it and now shes mad at me but shes still not coming back for like 2-3 days. But i also dont want to just leave the dog there to be hungry. Should i like take it to a shelter or call someone or something?? Idk

We both work fast food jobs and i have $30 to my name until monday when i get paid so no im not gonna waste my last few bucks on it i have to eat too😭 and she apparently spent all her money on the trip and told me if she sends me the money for it she doesnt know if she’ll have enough to get through the rest of the trip/get home bc she had to budget it really tightly

question: Based on this person's historical commenting patterns, which moral principle would they MOST likely use to judge this scenario?
answer_options: A. Property/Consent: The judgment is primarily justified by violations involving ownership, money, physical possessions, or bodily access without permission.
B. Care/Harm Prevention: The judgment is primarily justified by concern about direct physical or emotional suffering of a person (victim-centered empathy, trauma, distress).
C. Autonomy/Boundaries: The judgment is primarily justified by violation of personal sovereignty: controlling behavior, unwanted interference, privacy invasion, or boundary crossing (non-material).
D. Fairness/Reciprocity: The judgment is primarily justified by proportionality, equity, or reciprocal treatment (fair share, equal standards, tit-for-tat).

context.scenario:
My 35m car is in the shop right now so my wife 35f was giving me a ride to work today and taking our daughter 10f to school. 

When we got in the car and were about to go when our daughter got out of her booster seat. My wife and I told her to get back in it but she said she wasn't sitting in it, we told her she needed to sit in it and she needed to get in it right now because we had to go. 

She raised her voice and said " no I'm not sitting in that stupid booster seat you buttfaces" my wife kept trying to calmly tell her to get back in her booster seat but our daughter kept arguing. 

I became very irritated because I had to get to work and so did my wife and our daughter had to get to school and this little argument was gonna make everyone late. 

So turned to my daughter and told her if she didn't get back in her booster seat she would lose her iPad for 3 days. Aftter this she got back in her booster seat and buckled up. She didn't talk to us and had a grumpy look on her face the whole car ride. 

After we dropped her off at school my wife expressed how upset she was with me for telling our daughter she'd be punished all on my own. She said we are BOTH her parents and we need to discuss things on how to deal with her misbehavior together and it's not okay for one of us to just tell her she will be punished. 

I tried to explain to my wife that I didn't mean to upset her and we needed to get going and that's why i did it, this didn't make my wife feel better. 

When I got home from work I tried to see if she had gotten over it but she was still giving me the cold shoulder. I asked if she was still mad and she said of course she still was and did i think anger just evaporated? 

AITA?
context.comment:
NTA

Your wife is HALF correct. This was an incident where your child was being disrespectful and rude. That is 1,000% a moment where neither parent needs to discuss ANYTHING. You told your child that she was going to get punished if she didn't get back in her seat. Not only is this an acceptable threat, but the punishment fit the crime. 

What's your wife think is going to happen??? You're going to sit down and debate this shit every single time it happens?? She doesn't need (within reason) your permission to punish your daughter and neither do you. It's not as if you said "You're going to be grounded for 72 months" or some shit. You made a very reasonable threat of punishment for her actions. 

Further more, your wife is still angry after what I can only assume is a full EIGHT hours later?!? What's she think giving you the cold shoulder is gonna do??? That gonna fix the problem???

context.scenario:
I've had intestine problems for a while, nothing major, i just really have to go sometimes.
Mst twachers in my school know baiut this, as i filed in a doctors note.
Most of them don't have an issue with it, some of them even sat me near the exit, and said whenever i have to, i can just go, just to do so quietly, i got this type of trist because of my involvement in many school activities and representing the school in many events.
Recently, a new english teacher came to our school, i hate her, amd she knows it.
I simply cannot respect a teacher who doesn't know the material she's supposed to teach.
One time during her class, i was having an episode, and i excused myself.
She said im not allowed to go, i said i just have to, and she can ask the principal for the doctors notice as proof of my claim.
She said she will, but for now i have to stay.
Mind you, i was trying not to soil myself during this entire conversation.
So i was running out of patience and just said "are you gonna be the one to clean up if i shit myself?"
She was shocked and didn't say anything, i left for the bathroom.
I didn't face any disciplinary action, but she did.
My oarents were 50/50 on it, saying i could've handled the situation better, but that i did nothing wront.
My classmates and friends say that i pushed it and i should apologise.
So reddit, AITA?
context.comment:
NTA

I'll say this.... Your school's administration is an ah for not making sure the new teacher knew about your medical issues. While she's an ah, I will say she didn't know and it's hard to just believe a teenager at their word. I'd also say you might want to keep a copy of the letter with you at all times in case you end up with a sub or something. 

As for you talking about your English teacher "not knowing the material", you might want to make sure you shit is in order. When you're talking about yourself, it's I, not i. When you write/type the word English, it's always with a capital E.

context.scenario:
My husband is laid off work. He doesn’t have much going on right now. AITA for expecting him to:

- make dinner
- take the garbage out
- warm up my car in the AM
- make sure tires are ok in the AM
- drive me to work occasionally 
- put clothes away after I washed them

Am I?  I really don’t think these are unreasonable if you aren’t working at the moment. I question like why wouldn’t you want to do these things for me? Am I wrong? He gets so angry when I wake him up to warm up the car. 
————————
UPDATE: okay I get it I’m the a$$hole. I read a couple comments and am scared to read the rest 😂. 

However, I did leave out details:

- he hasn’t been working since beginning of February, so I started asking these things like mid-March because he plays game all day. 

- make dinner: I help prep most of the dinners on my weekend. So he has less to do on the weekdays. I still do dinner on the weekends. I always made dinner when we were both working.

- warm my car/check tires/take me to work: not everyday just when I’m running behind and because we live in a complex and have to park a block away.

But based on ya’all comments I’ll definitely try better to get ready on time in the morning. I admit I have taken my time lately to get ready because I know he’s not working. So yes, l am taking advantage of that.  I am the a$$hole, sadly.

I appreciate everyone’s insight. My husband appreciates your support. He thanks you and is laughing at me now.
context.comment:
YTA

I'm curious.... Are you arms and legs broken??? I can see chores around the house like cooking and cleaning... But warming the car for you ??? That's not something a SO does. That's something a servant does. I don't blame him. I would be fucking livid if someone woke me out of a sound sleep to do something they're perfectly capable of doing. Just an FYI, remote starters have been around since the mid 80's.

context.scenario:
Would I be the A-hole if my fiancee and I (a recovered alcoholic, 338 days sober) asked for my wedding to be a sober event? 

This seems like a simple one, yes it’s our wedding we make the rules right? Not that easy, both of our families are massive drinkers, the venue we have belongs to a family member (we don’t have the budget for anything else, and we are extremely grateful for the space) however it has a bar. I don’t think my family would take it well if I requested this, not the ones who own the venue necessarily, however all of my family are big drinkers in general so I feel it’s a big ask. I also feel like it’s rude to use my families space and then request this. A wedding these days seems to be an excuse for everyone to get drunk rather than a celebration of two people. but I really could not stand being at my own wedding with people who are extremely drunk. It’s off putting and triggering and that’s not so easy to explain to people who drink weekly socially. 

So WIBTA? Any advice on how to go about this in a way that might work?
context.comment:
NTA

This one is tricky. Cause the family member is likely offering you the space at a discount. So also putting a stop to the sale of booze might make them lose even more money. I also see why you'd want it booze free. Your SO is less than a year into sobriety. The only reason I'm questioning it is because the sale of booze at a wedding is big. Taking that away from the owner would suck if they're already doing you a favor.

context.scenario:
My girlfriend (22F) went to college to study computer science and graduated last year. She struggled to find a job for ages and only got one at the start of this year. 

Even when she was in college she used to express that she didn't know if she was a good programmer despite having decent grades.

I'm not in the same field and work as a simple bank teller but also do gig work like small home fixes and renos.

When she started the job she was surprised she got an offer and even admitted she thought that she got lucky because she is a woman and the company is trying to hire more women.

When she started she said she found the job hard even though she said the programming was quite low level.

3 months in and she seems to be struggling and I see her working sometimes till late in the night at home even when she returns from the office. I think by now she should have learned the ropes and shouldn't be struggling so much.

I mentioned this to her and said that perhaps this isn't the right career for her and that it is OK as plenty of people work different jobs than what they went to college for and even though I was just reminding her of the things she herself said about not feeling like she is a good developer she got upset at me and suddenly claimed she only works late since she enjoys it and wants to make a good early impression and get promoted when she is clearly struggling.

AITA here?
context.comment:
YTA

Basically, you're suggesting someone throw away 4 years of college due to their first year in the field not going perfectly. She's been there THREE months!! That is NOTHING in the grand scheme of things. She says she enjoys it. So let her keep doing it until she doesn't enjoy it anymore.

context.scenario:
My gf (26) and I (28) have been dating for two years. Now we would like to move in. We are currently in SoCal and living expenses is high out here

The amount that we’re seeing for rent is about $1800-2500. I don’t mind paying about $2k while my gf helps with atleast $1k or so for food, rent and utilities. 

Now she’s overreacting and saying how she doesn’t want to have the pressure of studying and going to school so she doesn’t wanna give the $1k. Maybe $500?

Regardless, am I the asshole for charging her? She’s saying that other people are supporting their gf without the pressure of bringing cash home.

 Further information, I got my career going and she is going to school for nursing.

UPDATE: People are asking the same questions:

She is in school for nursing. Income is weird since she is per diem. She takes shifts at a nursing home based on availability 

We discussed and she’s willing to provide 400-800. NOT 1k that I rounded up. She will work maybe 1 or 2 shifts per week or every other week. Depends on class schedules.

I am not in school anymore, I can afford to cover/provide for her. That way she can just focus on studies and we can reap the rewards later after she finishes. I think this is the right move

I just wanted people’s insights on the situation. I mean of course I will provide for my wife/fiancé but  we are not engaged yet; we are bf/gf but we’re in it for the long haul.
context.comment:
NTA

If it's too much for her, she can stay where she's living now/find her own place/move in with friends/family. Her statement about "others supporting their gf" makes it sound as if she thinks that her being female means she's entitled to hugely discounted rent if she is living with her bf.

context.scenario:
Posting on throwaway as I'm not sure if she will see this. A couple of days ago I (28M) and my wife(26F) went to a craft store. She found a good deal on large fluffy yarn. She has always said how she wants to make a big fluffy crochet blanket. So she got a few bundles. 

I learned how to crochet from my grandma years ago. My wife has seen me crochet before and knows I know how to do it. She had suggested that I could teach her how to crochet after I am off work this weekend. She works a different shift then I do so our schedules line up sporadically. I was more than happy to teach her. 

Today I got home from work and found the packaging in the trash. She worked a later shift today so I didn't bother her while she slept. When she woke up I saw that she had already started without me. She had been bored and decided she would try on her own. She said she messed it up after the first few rows and will need to start again. I was very upset and angry. 

I keep crocheting because my grandma has since past away so I feel close to her every time I'm working on a project. This cut deep emotionally. I feel betrayed and not like I was valued in this. She tried to downplay that it was a big deal and that I was overreacting because "I messed it up so I'll have to start over again" and "You can show me how to do it when I start over again". I was really looking forward to spending time with her and showing her a skill I learned from family. I'm confused if I did overreact and it's not as big as I am making it out to be. Any advice is very much appreciated. Thank you for taking the time to read. 

TL:DR My wife (26F) started crocheting from a video online after she asked me (28M) to show her how to do it as my grandma (who has passed) shower me how to.
context.comment:
YTA

Dude, your wife doesn't have to share a hobby with you. Start your own damn hobby. I mean come on... ANGRY?? Not even remotely close to a big enough deal to get angry over it. And for damn sure not something you YELL at someone for. 

Three words, bub.....

Anger. Management. Class

context.scenario:
My wife and I used to go to church regularly and were religious. Over time we both kind of stopped. Like, we stopped praying, reading the Bible, didn’t go to church anymore. It wasn’t a big argument or anything, it just faded out for both of us.

Now she’s suddenly decided she wants to start going again. She says she misses the religion and also wants the support system and to make friends. I told her I don’t really want to go. I don’t feel the same connection I used to and it would feel fake for me to sit there like I believe it all again. It’s just not where I’m at right now.

I’ve told her I’m down to do other things with her. I’ve offered to spend time together in other ways, I’ve even said I’ll keep our son at home while she goes, so she’s not juggling him during service. But she wants to take him, and now she’s upset that I’m not going because she says it makes her look bad going in alone with our kid.

It’s also not like it’s close. The church is about an hour away. I mentioned there’s local mom groups and community stuff here in our neighborhood where she could find support or friends too, but she’s stuck on this church.

Now she’s saying I’m selfish and childish, and that I’m not being a real man because I won’t go with her. It’s like nothing I offer matters unless I just say “fine” and go to church. But I don’t want to fake something I don’t believe in. I want to support her, but I don’t want to just fold and go do something that leaves me feeling fake or resentful.

AITA?


---

TL;DR: My wife wants to go back to church for religion and friends, but I’m not interested. I’ve offered other ways to support her but she’s upset I’m not going and says I make her look bad. Now I’m selfish and childish for not giving in. AITA?
context.comment:
NTA

Even if your wife isn't intentionally doing it, she's a giant ah for trying to force you into going to church. It is NEVER okay to force someone/guilt someone into practicing religion. If anyone here is being childish, it's her. She's TRIPLE an ah for then suggesting that you're "not being a real man" because you don't want to go to church with her. 

DO NOT give in to this!!

context.scenario: My gf (20f) and I (21m) have been together 4 years, and have been living together around 2 years. She had a part time job up until January or so, working 2-3 days per week, but she quit because she couldn't stand working there any longer. I on the other hand, work a full time job, and have a full time college schedule (though all the school is online, it still requires around 4 hours of work per week.) Anyways, I don't mind being "the provider," but I don't want some sort of trad wife who's only there to cook, clean, and look pretty. However, i feel as though if I'm putting in 40 hours a week in at work, and 4+ hours per week in to school, the least she could do is pick up around the house, (laundry, dishes, etc). I go to work 9-5, then we go to the gym, and then i come home and cook for us. Every time i try to express that our effort feels one sided, she says that I'm being unfair and that "she shouldn't be the only one doing things around the house," which i agree with to an extent. But she does nothing worthwhile while I'm at work. She'll sleep, lay in bed on her phone, or play video games. I just don't know how to work something out. Her friends also take her side and say it's unfair for me to ask her to clean, but I really don't understand how that's unreasonable to ask. Came here to rant more than anything, but AITA??
context.comment:
NTA

100% correct. Don't have a job?? It's your responsibility to take care of the house for a vast majority of the time. Shit, I'd LOVE to not work and my only responsibility was to take care of things around the house. I could literally be done "working" by noon every day!!

context.scenario:
So long story short, in the past my gf (me 27, her 26) admitted to using my razor on her coochie. I told her to never do that again and even gave her my old electric one to use. Fast forward to recent and I found out she used it again. Along with that, I have been telling her for months to stop showering without the fan on or door open a bit as it’ll cause water damage over time. Well, at the same time of finding out about the razor, she had made the bathroom walls wet with water again and I blew up a little bit. She has a hard time taking any criticism and has a hard time admitting fault so this went over not too great. Now a week or so later I was asking where something of mine was and if she possibly used it. Her response was, “why do you always assume I’m using your stuff???” I threw back, “says the person that was asked to not use my razor on her vag yet she did anyway!” Now for the first time she has decided to go spend the Saturday on her own instead of having me come with. I’m not worried we’re drifting apart, however I don’t want these things to keep happening without change.

For reference the ONLY thing we really bicker about is chores and who has done what (I really wish this would stop but we both keep on keeping track). We have also been together for about a year and 2 months. Any ladies or anyone else out there have any advice on how to get my lady to see my point of view or how to get her to be more reasonable to admitting fault/not being defensive all the time? Thanks.
context.comment:
NTA

First off, let me just say that I think it's beyond fucking gross to share razors with someone you're not married to. Even then, I still think it's nasty AF to share razors. Second, to use someone else's razor to shave your junk is not only disgusting, but it's disrespectful as all hell. 

Honestly, I'd dump someone that disrespected me like that.

context.scenario:
UPDATE: Thank you all for your feedback. Hard to not feel like the asshole when this happens, but I appreciate all of you helping me feel like I’m not just a jerk. I will read all of the comments, but I will not respond to them. If you want a response, please feel free to dm me, otherwise I really appreciate it. 

I (24f) generally maintain the house that me and my girlfriend (23/f) live in, and pay the majority of rent. We both work full time jobs, my girlfriend has only had her job for the past 2ish months and gets off later than me every day but one (we work roughly the same hours). On the topic of jobs, mine generally pays more, so we pay a fair percentage of rent, but I generally pay for groceries, dates, and most other things we need.

This is not new and with help I have gotten over frustrations about feeling like the split of chores, cooking, and expenses are heavier on me than her. But since she has had her job, I have made sure there is dinner ready, or 10 minutes out from being ready for her when she gets home. Every Tuesday, she gets off at 6pm and I get off at 8:30pm, and for the last two weeks, she has made dinner for herself, or eaten out and not gotten me anything. Before that, I was cooking for us when I got home around 9pm. These last two weeks, I have expressed that it feels unfair that I have dinner ready for her when she gets home 4/5 work days, and I cook and clean both weekend days.

To get to the point, tonight, I got home from work and was told my that girlfriend went to get fast food on her way home, but she got me a drink. I gave her the cold shoulder while I made my food, and she asked if I was mad. I told her I was frustrated that I keep having dinner ready for her every other work day, but the one day I get off really late, I have to make myself food, even though she feeds herself. She went to our bedroom where she has been crying since. I went to make sure she was okay after my food was cooked, and she told me that we (meaning I) need to create a meal plan so she knows what to cook for me, even though we never use a meal plan. Then I told her it’s frustrating, but we can move together as a team, only for her to tell me she had a shit day at work and that she thought she deserved fast food on the way home. Then I tried to ask about her day, to which she kept giving one word responses, or saying “it’s fine”. That ultimately led to me getting frustrated again, and leaving her alone cry in our bedroom.

I know I could have handled the situation better by asking how her day was before getting frustrated that there isn’t any dinner for me when I got home. However, I feel like I’ve made myself clear that it is frustrating to feel like the only one who cares in this relationship. And then to actively put aside how I’m feeling to ask her about her day, to only get one word answers back feels like a cop out. But I can’t help but feeling like the asshole as I’m eating my shitty instant ramen while she is actively crying in the room beside me…

AITA?
context.comment:
NTA

What she's doing is INSANELY rude. On multiple levels too!! I would be fucking LIVID if my SO was leaving me to fend for myself after I regularly cooked them meals/had food ready for them. The kicker is her trying to make it seem like you're an ah for expecting the same treatment she gets. I don't care how shitty your day/week is, when your SO regularly has dinner ready for you while also doing more chores around the house AND paying more of the bills, you make sure they're taken care of. What if you had a bad week?? What's it take?? 2 minutes to send a text letting you know to grab food for yourself if they're really that lazy??

context.scenario:
So, my daughter (a teen) recently got upset at me, because I didn’t do anything when she cried over food. For reference, what happened was, she went to the fridge to grab her food and it was already gone. So, she came into my room to ask where it went, and I admitted I gave it to her sibling. She then gets upset at me, and even though she doesn’t yell at me, she asks ‘why would you do that’, before going back to her own bedroom. 

I walk past a bit later, and I hear her crying and screaming into her pillow about how she goes to school all the time, works part time (which is true), and how she can never have anything in the house without someone else eating it. I admit, she did pay for it with her own money, however I don’t understand why she got so upset over it. She did come home from a 6 hour shift at her job, so I can get why she’s a bit more irritated, however i buy her food all the time, so she shouldn’t be upset over it. My other child said yeah, I am an asshole for giving her food to him, and he goes in to comfort her. Is it just teenagers being teenagers and getting upset over nothing, or am I genuinely an asshole?
context.comment:
YTA

Long story short, yeah, you're an ah. You gave away something that wasn't yours. It's kinda odd that you don't see how fucking annoying it is when you come home thinking about the leftovers you have only to find out someone ate it. She was probably lookin forward to that food for her entire shift at work. She's a teenager... They're emotional. So it stands to reason she would be frustrated enough to cry.

context.scenario:
My fiancée usually works from 2 PM to 11 PM and gets home around midnight or 1 AM. Last night, she ended up working much later than usual and didn’t get home until 4 AM.

When she got back, I asked her if she could let me know in the future if she’s working late. I explained that I wasn’t trying to control her or micromanage her time, but it’s common courtesy to let your partner know if there’s a major change in plans—especially since I was worried when she didn’t come home at her usual time.

Her response caught me off guard: she asked, “Why do you want me to inform you?” I didn’t expect that reaction, and it made me wonder if I was out of line for asking.

To me, it’s about respect and staying connected as partners, but I’m curious if others see it differently. AITA for expecting her to let me know if she’s working late?
context.comment:
NTA

You are 100% correct. It's common sense and common courtesy. Ask her if she'd prefer you report her missing/overdue. I can't help but wonder if she'd prefer the cops pulling her over to check on her/stopping at her place of work to check on her well being. Speaking as someone that works in public safety, people that refuse to do something as simple as this really annoy me. Fact of the matter is, police officers, firefighters, and EMS workers have a lot better things to be doing with their time. Having to search/check on someone because they don't feel like keeping their loved ones informed is just plain selfish. 

It's not as if you're asking/demanding that she check in every hour. You're asking her to call or text when she's going to be late coming home from work during the overnight hours.

context.scenario: I’m in my 30s my mother is in her mid 50s. She is Italian and a dramatic story teller who really drags out the point and often doesn’t let me get much dialogue in while she’s talking. It’s less of a back and forth and more of me listening to all the latest stuff she has to catch me up on. I speak to her often, and sometimes for a half hour or an hour at a time. She curses SO much, sometimes it feels like every other word especially if she’s catching me up on some drama. She will even tell a story saying this person said this blah blah and add in all these apparent curses they said. Example : Your uncle was like eff this and eff that. I ain’t dealing with this shiz any effing more. When I know this man doesn’t curse like that. It’s just been really irritating me and it’s been years. I cut her off mid conversation and said I can’t even listen to her stories anymore if she doesn’t cut out the cursing. I said it takes away from the entire things she’s trying to tell me. I mean I curse myself sometimes but when it’s warented like I’m frustrated not just story telling. She seemed embarrassed and offended. And pointed out that everyone does actually curse she’s not just adding it in. (Trust me she’s adding it in). Anyway she continued to curse seconds after I respectfully asked her that and I am just trying to think if I should say it again or just listen to this every convo forever. I also don’t want her cursing in front of my young kids and she does that too when she’s talking. I don’t even think she realizes.
context.comment:
YTA

You don't get to decide when it's "warranted" for someone else to swear. The only time you get to decide if someone is swearing is if it is in YOUR house. If you don't want her swearing in front of your kids, don't bring them around her or only have her around them in YOUR house where you can have a "no swearing" rule.

context.scenario:
So I (28M) live in a shared house with 4 other people. We each have our own food, but there’s an understanding that if you really need to borrow something—milk for coffee, a slice of bread, whatever—you can, as long as you’re respectful about it.

The issue is, whenever I do borrow something, I make sure to pay for exactly what I took. For example, if I use 1/4 cup of someone’s milk (which is 1/64th of a gallon), and the milk cost $3.00, I’ll leave 17 cents on the counter, usually in coins. Same thing if I use a tablespoon of peanut butter—I weigh it out, do the math, and leave the equivalent change behind. I figure that’s fair, right?

Well, my housemates think I’m being weird and petty. One of them said it’s “creepy” how precise I am, and another said it makes them feel “nickel and dimed” even though I’m literally paying for what I took. One even asked me to just not pay and either leave a note or replace the item when it’s low, saying the coins on the counter are annoying and make the kitchen look like a toll booth.

But in my mind, I’m being respectful and making sure I’m not freeloading. I don’t see how it’s different than someone borrowing gas and paying you back for it. So now I’m stuck—should I stop doing it this way?

AITA for leaving exact change instead of replacing the item or asking every time?
context.comment:
YTA

I wouldn't call it creepy as much as I'd call it SUPER cheap skate like. I mean dude.... If you borrow milk, replace it. Who in the entire hell measures fucking peanut butter?!? lmfao!! and 17 cents??? WTF are they supposed to do with that?? Your roommate is right. You're nickle and diming them. I get wanting to save money, but good lord. 

FYI, when you "borrow" someone's food, they have to replace it. This means walking/driving to go get it. So that 17 cents aint fully covering it.

context.scenario:
My wife and I usually end up having my mother-in-law share a room with us while our family is on vacation. The issue with that is my mother-in-law snores super loud and it affects my sleep. I've complained about it, and my wife continues to have her mother stay with us on vacations.

AITA for asking my wife to not have her mother stay with us on vacations?

Edit: To clarify, we go on vacations with the whole family, which is about 20 people. The other people in the family don't have room for her mother, so we end up having her in our room.

Edit #2: Thank you everyone for your comments, they helped open my eyes a bit.

I spoke with my wife and said "the next time we book a trip, can you please ask me if you want your mom or dad to stay with us?" She immediately started yelling at me, cursed me out, and hung up on me.

Not a great conversation, but I'm glad I could express myself.
context.comment:
NTA

I'm very intrigued by this "We go on vacation with (20 people) the whole family and NO ONE seems to have any room for mom" shit. By that, I mean I don't buy it for a second. Your family is lying to you. There's not a chance in hell the don't have the room. 

If I'm you, the next time you go on vacation, I'd get a room by myself and your wife and share the room with her mother.

scenario:
I (24F) work as a receptionist at a busy medical Center. I’ve been there for 3 years and am pretty good at my job. However in the past few months with new staff, a new system and working less —> making a few mistakes with booking. These are due to lapse of focus and are generally quite easy to fix (double booking, booking the wrong room for an appointment etc.) 

It’s my fault entirely - I take full responsibility. This is no excuse per se, but more reasoning for why these mistakes happen. Our booking system is quite confusing - for example the type of appointments we offer on different days constantly changes, the interface is really slow so sometimes your changes don’t save, etc.

Anyways the long and short is I made a stupid mistake at a rate of say one per week. 

My boss makes sure I know of this. Every single mistake I make she send me a long paragraph, detailing the mistake and reminding me “of how long I’ve been here for” and that “I should know this by now” and that “I keep making mistakes”. It’s annoying but I usually just take it on the chin and move on. It’s my fault anyways so I just fix the issue and avoid doing it again. 

My issue is that recently she has started to undermine my character while giving this “feedback”. I’m currently studying to be a doctor, and she frequently mentions “you’re going to be a doctor one day” “if you make mistakes like this when you’re a doctor they will cancel your registration” and other snide comments about my character/intelligence/competence and how it will affect my future career. It’s getting to the point where I’m starting to tear up, even tho I try not to let it affect me. 

Apart from this constant criticism she is a good boss. She is accomodating, reasonable, it pays well and she even gives us gifts for the holidays. It’s JUST her inability to be kind when she criticises me, or even give any sort of praise for when I do the right thing, which I have a problem with. 

I recently came across a well written article on a blog called “7 tips for providing constructive feedback to your team”. I think it would genuinely be really helpful for her to read. Also, I haven’t responded to her latest critique session of me and I think this would be a nice/helpful response. Though I’m worried it will come across as passive aggressive 

Link to the article is below

https://www.mural.co/blog/constructive-feedback

I would really appreciate any suggestions on how to approach this situation. Sometimes I find it hard to understand people and big emotions I feel like this, so any guidance is hugely appreciated. Thank you
question: Based on this person's historical commenting patterns, which moral principle would they MOST likely use to judge this scenario?
answer_options: A. Fairness/Reciprocity: The judgment is primarily justified by proportionality, equity, or reciprocal treatment (fair share, equal standards, tit-for-tat).
B. Autonomy/Boundaries: The judgment is primarily justified by violation of personal sovereignty: controlling behavior, unwanted interference, privacy invasion, or boundary crossing (non-material).
C. Role-based Responsibility: The judgment is primarily justified by duties tied to a specific functional role (tenant/roommate/employee/co-parent) rather than emotion-based loyalty.
D. Property/Consent: The judgment is primarily justified by violations involving ownership, money, physical possessions, or bodily access without permission.
